Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), mild liver enzyme elevation (ALT), and abnormal urine findings (glucosuria, ketonuria, proteinuria, low specific gravity, brown color, ammoniacal odor). These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy and/or acute kidney injury, with no evidence of urinary tract infection. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
